name: CALM 1.2 Interfaces Reference
description: A reference architecture demonstrating both interface forms available in CALM 1.2: the freeform interface-type (no definition-url) and the formal interface-definition (definition-url + config). Interfaces live on NODES, not on relationships. Relationships reference interface unique-ids via source.interfaces and destination.interfaces arrays. No 1.0-rc1 url-interface or host-port-interface types are used here.

nodes.name: Analyst
nodes.description: Human user who queries the orders system via the analyst portal.

nodes.name: Analyst Portal
nodes.description: Browser-based web application through which the analyst accesses the orders system.

nodes.name: Orders API
nodes.description: Backend REST service exposing order management operations. Demonstrates two interface forms: a freeform interface-type (unique-id + free properties) and a formal interface-definition (definition-url + config).

nodes.name: Orders Database
nodes.description: Relational database storing order records, accessible only by the Orders API via JDBC.

relationships.description: Analyst accesses the orders portal to submit and monitor order activity.

Analyst portal calls the Orders API over HTTPS. The source.interfaces references the freeform interface on analyst-portal; destination.interfaces references the freeform REST interface on orders-api. This demonstrates how relationships reference interface unique-ids to specify which interfaces participate in the connection.
Orders API reads and writes order records to the Orders Database via JDBC.
